meta.title: Human papillomavirus type 16 recombinant DNA is maintained as an autonomously replicating episome in monkey kidney cells -- Chesters and McCance 66 (3): 615 -- Journal of General Virology

abstractHtml: Human papillomavirus type 16 (HPV16) DNA cloned in the expression vector pSV2-neo has been shown to be maintained in transfected monkey kidney cells as an autonomously replicating episome at a level of 2 to 10 copies per cell. Integration of pSV2-neo/HPV16 DNA with the host genome occurred in transfected mouse fibroblasts. Neither type of cell appeared to be phenotypically transformed.
